title: Bytes, Object Representation, Alignment, Padding, and Endianness · Memory Trace: Explicit Clearing
summary: A device protocol works on one architecture but leaks padding and faults on a stricter-alignment target. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem explicit clearing increment in portable binary representation codec. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use C23 memset_explicit or a reviewed non-elidable alternative for secrets and verify compiler and library support.
Encode and decode external integers byte-by-byte or with defined conversion APIs instead of dumping native objects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Explicit Clearing · Theory Explicit Clearing
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 4793. Produce bounded C evidence to encode values without copying native padding; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
